name: Verify and Falsify
content:
Review recent work with fresh context, try to falsify it, fix clear in-scope issues, and align affected documentation before the work is considered done.

Assume I have just completed work and want an objective verification, repair, and documentation-alignment pass, ideally from a new context window or outside model.

## Goals

- Explain what was developed in plain English.
- Verify the implementation against the intended criteria, spec, or requested behavior.
- Look for weaknesses, mismatches, omissions, broken assumptions, regressions, rule violations, downstream issues, and edge cases.
- Make focused fixes when the correct fix is clear, safe, and within scope.
- Align affected downstream documentation when recent changes make docs, help text, specs, tasks, or workflow references stale.
- Report what was verified, fixed, documented, and still risky.

## Review and Repair Rules

Analyze the recent changes and related spec or intended behavior objectively. Look for inconsistencies between implementation and criteria, plus broken tests, invalid JSON/Markdown, stale command descriptions, missing linked documentation, acceptance-criteria gaps, or obvious downstream breakage.

When a fix is directly supported by context, implement it immediately instead of only reporting it. Keep fixes minimal, targeted, and consistent with the existing codebase or artifact style. Do not rewrite unrelated areas or expand scope because of adjacent improvements.

If an issue is real but cannot be fixed safely from available context, report it clearly rather than guessing. Stop and report instead of making fixes that require product decisions, broad refactors, destructive operations, credentials, external access, or unclear intent.

Run or recommend the most relevant verification after fixes when possible. Preserve user changes and unrelated work.

## Documentation Alignment

Treat downstream documentation QA as part of this same pass, not as a separate default follow-up. Check affected README files, docs, workflow or quick-help text, specs, tasks, decision notes, project cards, or similar project documents only when the recent change touched behavior, user-facing commands, prompt chains, workflow state, acceptance criteria, or documented implementation details.

Update stale documentation when the right change is clear and in scope. Check for stale prompt-chain hints, quick-help entries, trigger references, command names, or template descriptions when workflow or prompt changes affect them. If documentation impact is ambiguous or broad, report the risk and the specific files that may need human review instead of guessing.

## Final Response

Start with what you verified and what you fixed. Then explain the recent work in plain English, list fixes grouped by file or concern, list documentation updated or confirmed current, list verification performed and results, and list remaining issues or follow-up only if they still exist. Keep the summary direct and evidence-based.

--- Secondary ---

Include quality-control checks for oversized files, large diffs, or other signs that changes may have introduced hidden problems when useful. If useful, frame the review as if performed by an outside model with no prior attachment to the work.

--- Optional YOLO Ship Behavior ---

This section is optional and easy to delete. Use it only when the user explicitly asks for yolo ship, ship it, commit and push, merge, or an equivalent completion path. Do not treat it as default verification behavior.

Before committing, pushing, opening a PR, merging, deleting branches, tagging, or publishing, confirm the repository context is safe: relevant verification passed, the diff is understood, unrelated user changes are preserved, credentials and remotes are available, and branch or review rules do not block the action. If safe and explicitly requested, complete the requested ship path with focused commit messages and report the exact commands or platform actions taken. If not safe, stop after verification and report the manual step needed.
description: Fresh-context verification, repair, and documentation-alignment pass that fixes clear issues and summarizes remaining risk.
